note: Consolidated alchm model. Single source: the spec's tables and any TypeScript constants are generated FROM this file. A table appearing in two places is a defect.

provenanceTags.CANVAS: Transcribed from the Alchm Semantics workbook. Cite the tab.
provenanceTags.CODE: Resolved in implementation, promoted here. Cite file:line.
provenanceTags.AUTHORED: Decided during completion. Reasoning in the spec.
provenanceTags.DERIVED: Computed from other values. No independent information.
provenanceTags.OPEN: Unresolved. Listed in the spec's gap register.

runtimeSlots.SIGN_ELEMENT_OF_OTHER: Resolves to the element of the other body's sign placement at computation time. The canvas writes this as 'x'. NOT missing data.
runtimeSlots.SIGN_ELEMENT_OF_SELF: The canvas writes this as 'y'. Undefined anywhere in the workbook; treated as the self-side counterpart of x. [OPEN]

aspectGrids.note: Lower triangle and upper triangle are DIFFERENT aspects. See SYNTHESIS_MODEL.md §5.

cells.flagReason: Contains Ground, an element neither planet carries. Probable slip; the fitted rule adjudicates. See §5b.
Byte-identical to Saturn×Sun above it and contains Ground, which is Saturn's element not Uranus's. Probable fill-down slip. See §5b.
Only genuinely empty cell in any grid.

cells.correctionReason: Fill-down from the Jupiter row directly above (9/11 cells byte-identical, and the two planets' ESMS pairs differ, so identity can only be a copy). Essence is Jupiter's axis; Saturn is Spirit/Matter. Corrected by substituting Saturn's own second axis, Matter, which restores the synthesis-pool constraint to 100%. Canvas value preserved below. See SYNTHESIS_MODEL.md §5b, §12.

Fill-down from the Jupiter row directly above (9/11 cells byte-identical, and the two planets' ESMS pairs differ, so identity can only be a copy). Essence is Jupiter's axis; Saturn is Spirit/Matter. Corrected by substituting Saturn's own second axis, Matter, which restores the synthesis-pool constraint to 100%. Canvas value preserved below. See SYNTHESIS_MODEL.md §5b, §12.
